type_error_policy: Pycairo runtime TypeError assertions map to MoonBit compile-time rejection. Path is an abstract opaque snapshot returned by Context copy operations and has no public constructor. Heterogeneous Python object comparisons are unavailable statically; Path-to-Path equality, ordering, and hashing preserve cairo path pointer identity.

static_api: pub fn Context::copy_path(Self) -> Path raise CairoError
test_path.adaptation: Path is an abstract opaque snapshot obtained from Context::copy_path or Context::copy_path_flat, so direct construction is rejected at compile time.

static_api: pub fn Path::to_string(Self) -> String raise CairoError
test_path_str.adaptation: MoonBit uses the explicit to_string method; it preserves pycairo's line-oriented command spelling and six-decimal coordinate formatting.

test_path_compare_hash.adaptation: Path-to-Path equality, ordering, and hashing use the wrapped cairo_path_t identity, matching pycairo. Comparisons with unrelated dynamic objects are rejected statically.

static_api: pub fn Path::iter(Self) -> Iter[PathSegment] raise CairoError
test_path_iter.adaptation: Iteration yields a closed PathSegment enum. components exposes the pycairo-compatible (PathDataType, coordinates) view without dynamic tuples.
